post.description: Delete every config, flag, logger, log group, context, context type, environment, and customer API key (except the caller's current key) on the account. The `common` config is preserved as a structural anchor but its items are reset. Requires `OWNER` role and a body of `{"confirm": true}` — any other value returns 400. get.description:
Return distinct values observed for a single attribute across context instances of one context type. The intended use case is a typeahead picker in a rule-building UI: the customer chooses a context type and an attribute name, then this endpoint streams back the distinct values matching what they've typed so far.

`filter[context_type]` and `filter[attribute]` are required. `filter[attribute]` accepts any attribute name — including the two first-class columns `key` and `name` — and is treated uniformly from the customer's perspective; the server adjusts the underlying query accordingly.

`filter[search]` does a case-insensitive starts-with match. The returned set excludes empty strings and NULL values.

invoices being visible at once.

get.description:
Return a single invoice by id. Supports content negotiation via the
`Accept` header:

- `application/pdf` — streams the invoice PDF.
- `application/vnd.api+json`, `application/json`, or absent — returns
  the JSON:API invoice resource.
- Any other value — `406 Not Acceptable`.

200.description: Successful response. Content type depends on the Accept header: application/vnd.api+json returns a JSON:API invoice resource; application/pdf streams the invoice PDF from Stripe.

406.description: Accept header specifies an unsupported media type

post.description:
Create a Stripe SetupIntent for adding a payment method without an
immediate charge. Returns the `client_secret` to pass to Stripe Elements
in the browser.

get.description: Return the SSO connection for the current account, including computed Service Provider metadata. Returns `404` when no connection has been configured.

put.description: Create-or-replace the account's SSO connection. The OIDC `client_secret` is write-only; supply it on first creation, omit on subsequent updates to retain the stored value.

delete.description: Soft-delete the account's SSO connection. Domains are preserved.

get.description: List the domains claimed by the current account, including each row's verification status.

put.description: Claim a domain for SSO routing. Idempotent — re-claiming a domain already held by the account is a no-op success. The response includes the DNS TXT token to publish.

post.description: Run the DNS TXT check for a claimed domain. Returns the updated resource on success. Returns `409` if a different account has already verified this domain. Returns `422` when the DNS TXT record has not yet been published or does not match the expected token.

key.title: Key
key.description: Stable URL-safe identifier for the account, derived from the account name at creation. Used in console URLs and other places that prefer a human-readable handle.

has_stripe_customer.title: Has Stripe Customer
has_stripe_customer.description: `true` once the account has been linked to a billing provider customer record.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: When the account is scheduled to expire. `null` for accounts with no expiry.

deleted_at.title: Deleted At
deleted_at.description: When the account was deleted. `null` for active accounts.

product_subscriptions.title: Product Subscriptions
product_subscriptions.description: Map of product key to the account's subscription summary for that product, including plan, status, and entitlement limits.

entry_point.title: Entry Point
entry_point.description: How the account first reached smplkit (e.g. `LOGIN`, `GET_STARTED`, `LIVE_DEMO`).

show_sample_data.title: Show Sample Data
show_sample_data.description: Whether the account is currently configured to display the sample dataset alongside the customer's own resources.

discount_override_pct.title: Discount Override Pct
discount_override_pct.description: Custom discount percentage applied to the account in place of the volume-based discount schedule. `null` means the volume schedule applies. Who set it, when, and why are captured in the audit-event stream rather than on the subscription row.

Account.title: Account
Account.description:
A tenant of smplkit — the unit of isolation that owns all of a
customer's resources (environments, contexts, API keys, and so on).

AccountRequest.title: AccountRequest
AccountRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating an account.

AccountResource.title: AccountResource
AccountResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for an account.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

AccountResponse.title: AccountResponse
AccountResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for an account.

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description: Must be `true` for the wipe to proceed. Any other value returns 400.

generate_sample_data.title: Generate Sample Data
generate_sample_data.description: When `true`, re-seed the account with the standard sample dataset after wiping. Best-effort: any seeding failure is logged but does not fail the wipe.

AccountWipeRequest.title: AccountWipeRequest
AccountWipeRequest.description: Confirmation envelope for the wipe-account action.

stripe_payment_method_id.title: Stripe Payment Method Id
stripe_payment_method_id.description: Identifier of the Stripe payment method to register on the account, e.g. `pm_1234567890abcdef`.

default.title: Default
default.description: When `true`, make the newly registered payment method the account's default. The first payment method on an account is always set as default regardless of this field.

AddPaymentMethodAttributes.title: AddPaymentMethodAttributes
AddPaymentMethodAttributes.description:
Attributes accepted when registering a new payment method.

The customer first creates a Stripe payment method client-side using
Stripe Elements, then submits its `pm_...` identifier here to persist
it on the account.

AddPaymentMethodBody.title: AddPaymentMethodBody
AddPaymentMethodBody.description: JSON:API request envelope for registering a new payment method.

AddPaymentMethodData.title: AddPaymentMethodData
AddPaymentMethodData.description: Resource object for the add-payment-method request.

AdminSubscriptionRequest.title: AdminSubscriptionRequest
AdminSubscriptionRequest.description: Admin-scope request envelope for replacing a subscription.

items.title: Items
items.description: Desired enrollments. Products listed are scheduled to be on the specified plan immediately (for upgrades and new enrollments) or at the end of the current billing period (for downgrades). Products not listed are scheduled to be dropped at the end of the current billing period.

payment_method.title: Payment Method
payment_method.description: Optional identifier of the payment method to bill against. If omitted, the account's default payment method is used.

discount_override_pct.title: Discount Override Pct
discount_override_pct.description: Administrator-set discount percentage (0–100). When set, the multi-product discount schedule is bypassed and this value is used directly. Setting `100` skips the billing provider entirely — the customer pays nothing. Pass `null` to clear any existing override and revert to the multi-product discount schedule.

AdminSubscriptionRequestAttributes.title: AdminSubscriptionRequestAttributes
AdminSubscriptionRequestAttributes.description: Same as the customer request body plus the admin-only override field.

AdminSubscriptionRequestResource.title: AdminSubscriptionRequestResource
AdminSubscriptionRequestResource.description: Admin-scope resource object for a subscription update request.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle state of the key. `ACTIVE` keys may be used to authenticate; `REVOKED` keys are rejected.

key.title: Key
key.description: The bearer token value. Returned in plaintext on the create response so the caller can capture it; subsequent reads return the same value for round-tripping.

scopes.title: Scopes
scopes.description: Scope restrictions applied to the key. Empty object grants full account access; populated forms are reserved for future scope syntax.

created_by.title: Created By
created_by.description: UUID of the user who created the key.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Optional expiry timestamp. After this time, the key is rejected. Omit for keys that do not expire.

last_used_at.title: Last Used At
last_used_at.description: When the key was most recently used to authenticate.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the key was last modified.

ApiKey.title: ApiKey
ApiKey.description:
An API key used by SDKs, scripts, and other programmatic clients to
authenticate with the smplkit API on behalf of the account.

The full key value is returned in plaintext on the create response and
is otherwise unavailable — record it somewhere safe immediately after
creation.

protocol.description: Federation protocol. `oidc` for OpenID Connect; `saml` for SAML 2.0. Determines which set of IdP fields below are required.

oidc_issuer.description: OIDC issuer URL — the base from which `.well-known/openid-configuration` is discovered. Required when `protocol` is `oidc`; ignored when `protocol` is `saml`.

oidc_client_id.description: OIDC client identifier issued by the IdP for smplkit. Required when `protocol` is `oidc`; ignored otherwise.

oidc_client_secret.description: OIDC client secret. Write-only — supplied on PUT, never returned by the API. Stored envelope-encrypted at rest. Required on first creation of an OIDC connection; on subsequent PUTs, omit to retain the existing value.

saml_idp_entity_id.title: Saml Idp Entity Id
saml_idp_entity_id.description: SAML IdP EntityID (typically a URI). Required when `protocol` is `saml`; ignored otherwise.

saml_idp_slo_url.description: SAML IdP single logout URL. Optional — when present, smplkit will issue LogoutRequests on user sign-out.

saml_idp_x509_cert.title: Saml Idp X509 Cert

group_role_mappings.title: Group Role Mappings
group_role_mappings.description: Mapping of IdP group claim values to smplkit roles. The first key matching the user's group claims (in declaration order) decides the JIT role; if none match, `default_role` applies. Example: `{"smplkit-admins": "ADMIN"}`.

enforced.title: Enforced
enforced.description: When `true`, password and social sign-in are rejected for users whose email domain matches one of the account's verified domains. The account owner is exempt (break-glass).

acs_url.title: Acs Url

slo_url.title: Slo Url
slo_url.description: Single Logout URL to register with the IdP. Computed; smplkit accepts logout requests here for the SAML case.

SSOConnection.title: SSOConnection
SSOConnection.description:
An account's Single Sign-On connection to a customer-controlled
identity provider. Configuring a connection lets the account federate
authentication to its own SAML or OIDC IdP; with `enforced` enabled,
password and social sign-in are disabled for users on the account's
verified domains.

Each account has at most one SSO connection. The Service Provider
metadata fields (`sp_entity_id`, `acs_url`, `slo_url`) are computed
on every read from the connection identifier and never stored.

SSOConnectionRequest.title: SSOConnectionRequest
SSOConnectionRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or replacing the SSO connection.

SSOConnectionResource.title: SSOConnectionResource
SSOConnectionResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for an SSO connection.

`id` is the server-assigned UUID of the singleton connection row on
the account; clients never specify it (the URL is
`/accounts/current/sso_connection` with no id segment).

SSOConnectionResponse.title: SSOConnectionResponse
SSOConnectionResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for the SSO connection.

dns_txt_token.title: Dns Txt Token
dns_txt_token.description: Token to publish on the domain's DNS as a TXT record to prove ownership. The full record value is `smplkit-domain-verification=<token>`.

verified_at.title: Verified At
verified_at.description: When the domain was verified. Null until verification succeeds.

status.title: Status
status.description: Verification status. `pending` means a claim has been registered but DNS TXT verification has not yet succeeded. `verified` means the domain is in use for SSO routing.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the claim was last modified.

SSODomain.title: SSODomain
SSODomain.description:
An email domain claimed by an account for SSO routing. A domain is
not active until it has been verified by publishing a DNS TXT record
containing the `dns_txt_token`. Once verified, sign-in attempts on
that domain route to this account's SSO connection.

Verified-domain ownership is global across accounts — two accounts
cannot both hold the same verified domain.

SSODomainListResponse.title: SSODomainListResponse
SSODomainListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for SSO domains on an account.

SSODomainRequest.title: SSODomainRequest
SSODomainRequest.description:
JSON:API request envelope for claiming a domain.

The attributes block is intentionally optional/empty — all writeable
data on a domain is the domain string itself (in the URL) and the
server-generated verification token. Claim is idempotent: re-claim
of a domain the account already holds is a no-op success.

SSODomainResource.title: SSODomainResource
SSODomainResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for an SSO domain.

The resource `id` is the domain string itself per ADR-035 (e.g.
`"acme.com"`). The domain is normalised to lower-case on write.

SSODomainResponse.title: SSODomainResponse
SSODomainResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for an SSO domain.

prorated_charge_today_cents.title: Prorated Charge Today Cents
prorated_charge_today_cents.description: When `effect` is `IMMEDIATE`, the estimated prorated charge for the remainder of the current billing period in cents. Always `0` when `effect` is `NEXT_PERIOD`.

starts_at.title: Starts At
starts_at.description: When `effect` is `NEXT_PERIOD`, the ISO-8601 timestamp at which the change takes effect. `null` when `effect` is `IMMEDIATE` (the change applies on confirmation).

SubscriptionChangeProjection.title: SubscriptionChangeProjection
SubscriptionChangeProjection.description: Per-item projected effect of a subscription change.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Target plan for this product. Must be a paid plan such as `standard` or `pro`; the free plan is implicit when a product is not listed.

SubscriptionItemRequest.title: SubscriptionItemRequest
SubscriptionItemRequest.description:
One product enrollment as supplied by the caller.

The caller supplies the *desired* (product, plan) pair for each product
they want enrolled. Products absent from the request are interpreted as
scheduled-for-drop at the end of the current billing period.

price_monthly_cents.title: Price Monthly Cents
price_monthly_cents.description: Monthly list price for this enrollment, in cents. This value is locked at the time the enrollment was created or last had its plan changed; subsequent changes to the public price list do not affect this enrollment until the customer themselves changes their plan.

pending_plan_change.title: Pending Plan Change
pending_plan_change.description: When a plan change is scheduled for the end of the current billing period, this is the plan that will take effect. Otherwise `null`. The value `free` indicates the enrollment will be dropped.

scheduled_change_effective_at.title: Scheduled Change Effective At
scheduled_change_effective_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at which the pending plan change takes effect. Matches the subscription's `current_period_end`.

SubscriptionItemResponse.title: SubscriptionItemResponse
SubscriptionItemResponse.description: One product enrollment as exposed in subscription responses.

projected_subtotal_cents.title: Projected Subtotal Cents
projected_subtotal_cents.description: Projected sum of item monthly list prices after the change.

projected_discount_pct.title: Projected Discount Pct
projected_discount_pct.description: Projected discount percentage that will apply after the change.

projected_discount_source.title: Projected Discount Source
projected_discount_source.description: `VOLUME` when the projected discount comes from the multi-product schedule; `OVERRIDE` when an administrator's discount applies.

projected_discount_amount_cents.title: Projected Discount Amount Cents
projected_discount_amount_cents.description: Projected discount amount in cents after the change.

projected_total_cents.title: Projected Total Cents
projected_total_cents.description: Projected final monthly total in cents after the change.

projected_next_tier.description: Hint describing additional savings available by subscribing to another product after the change is applied. `null` when no better tier is reachable.

changes.title: Changes
changes.description: Per-product breakdown of changes the desired state would produce. Products that would remain unchanged are omitted.

total_charge_today_cents.title: Total Charge Today Cents
total_charge_today_cents.description: Total amount that would be charged at confirmation time, in cents. The sum of `prorated_charge_today_cents` across `IMMEDIATE` changes.

next_invoice_total_cents.title: Next Invoice Total Cents
next_invoice_total_cents.description: Projected total of the next monthly invoice in cents, after all scheduled changes have taken effect.

SubscriptionPreviewAttributes.title: SubscriptionPreviewAttributes
SubscriptionPreviewAttributes.description: Projected totals and per-change breakdown for a hypothetical change.

SubscriptionPreviewResource.title: SubscriptionPreviewResource
SubscriptionPreviewResource.description: JSON:API resource object for a subscription preview.

SubscriptionPreviewResponse.title: SubscriptionPreviewResponse
SubscriptionPreviewResponse.description: Response envelope for the preview action.

SubscriptionRequest.title: SubscriptionRequest
SubscriptionRequest.description: Single-resource request envelope for replacing the subscription.

items.title: Items
items.description: Desired enrollments. Products listed are scheduled to be on the specified plan immediately (for upgrades and new enrollments) or at the end of the current billing period (for downgrades). Products not listed are scheduled to be dropped at the end of the current billing period.

payment_method.title: Payment Method
payment_method.description: Optional identifier of the payment method to bill against. If omitted, the account's default payment method is used.

id.title: Id
id.description: Subscription identifier; the server ignores this and uses the auth context.

SubscriptionRequestResource.title: SubscriptionRequestResource
SubscriptionRequestResource.description: JSON:API resource object for a subscription update request.

id.title: Id
id.description: Subscription identifier. Always `current` on response; absent on create-style requests.

SubscriptionResource.title: SubscriptionResource
SubscriptionResource.description: JSON:API resource object for a subscription.

SubscriptionResponse.title: SubscriptionResponse
SubscriptionResponse.description: Single-resource response envelope for a subscription.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle state of the subscription. `ACTIVE` while billing is current; `PAST_DUE` after a failed charge; `CANCELED` once the subscription has ended; `null` when the subscription is fully discounted (`discount_override_pct` of 100) and has no billing-provider object.

current_period_start.title: Current Period Start
current_period_start.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the current billing period's start.

current_period_end.title: Current Period End
current_period_end.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the current billing period's end. Scheduled plan changes take effect at this moment.

discount_pct.title: Discount Pct
discount_pct.description: Effective discount percentage applied to the subscription's monthly invoice. This is the value locked at the time of the customer's last subscription change; subsequent changes to the public discount schedule do not affect this customer until they themselves change their subscription.

discount_source.title: Discount Source
discount_source.description: `VOLUME` when the discount comes from the multi-product discount schedule; `OVERRIDE` when an administrator has applied a custom discount.

subtotal_cents.title: Subtotal Cents
subtotal_cents.description: Sum of all item list prices in cents, before discount.

discount_amount_cents.title: Discount Amount Cents
discount_amount_cents.description: Amount discounted from the subtotal in cents.

total_cents.title: Total Cents
total_cents.description: Final monthly total in cents after the discount is applied.

next_tier.description: When `discount_source` is `VOLUME` and the customer is below the top tier, describes the additional savings available by subscribing to another product. `null` otherwise.

payment_method.title: Payment Method
payment_method.description: Identifier of the default payment method used to bill this subscription. `null` when the subscription has no associated payment method (e.g. fully discounted via `discount_override_pct` of 100).

items.title: Items
items.description: One entry per product currently enrolled on the subscription.

SubscriptionResponseAttributes.title: SubscriptionResponseAttributes
SubscriptionResponseAttributes.description: Customer's subscription as returned by the API.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address used to sign in to the user account.

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the console and on shared resources.

profile_pic.title: Profile Pic
profile_pic.description: URL of an external profile picture (e.g. the value supplied by the user's identity provider).

avatar_url.title: Avatar Url
avatar_url.description: Server-generated `data:` URL containing the user's avatar image bytes when one has been captured. `null` when no avatar is available — callers should fall back to Gravatar or initials.

auth_provider.title: Auth Provider
auth_provider.description: Identity provider that authenticates the user, e.g. `google`, `microsoft`, or `email`.

email_verified.title: Email Verified
email_verified.description: Whether the user has completed email verification.

role.title: Role
role.description: Role the user holds in the current account context. One of `OWNER`, `ADMIN`, `MEMBER`, or `VIEWER`.

account.title: Account
account.description: UUID of the account the user is acting within.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the user record was created.

User.title: User
User.description: A person with access to one or more accounts in smplkit.

UserRequest.title: UserRequest
UserRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating a user.

UserResource.title: UserResource
UserResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a user.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

UserResponse.title: UserResponse
UserResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a user.

token.title: Token
token.description: Verification token previously delivered to the user's email.

HTTPBearer.description: JWT access token or API key (sk_api_*)
